seoTitle: Add Icons to Buttons in Webflow
metaDescription: How to build a custom icon-and-text button in Webflow using a Link Block, Flexbox alignment, and an SVG icon element.
intro: Plain text buttons can only get you so far when you want a more engaging call to action. Webflow lets you build a custom icon button by combining a Link Block, Flexbox alignment, and a nested SVG icon alongside your text. This guide walks through the whole structural setup, including hover states.

steps.heading: Add a Link Block
steps.body: Drag a Link Block container onto your page instead of a standard text button, from the Basic Elements section of the Add panel.

steps.heading: Set up Flexbox alignment
steps.body: Give the container a descriptive class name, then set its display property to Flex with horizontal and center alignment.

steps.heading: Add your text
steps.body: Drag a Text Block inside the link, type your call to action, and strip away the default underline styling.

steps.heading: Add the icon
steps.body: Drag an Image or Icon element next to the text, then upload a scalable SVG graphic from your Assets tray.

steps.heading: Adjust spacing and add a hover state
steps.body: Set a fixed width on the icon and a margin to create spacing, then use the States dropdown to change the background color on hover.

faq.question: Why use a Link Block instead of a regular button element?
faq.answer: A Link Block gives you more flexibility to nest multiple elements like text and icons together inside one clickable container.

faq.question: Should I use SVG or PNG icons?
faq.answer: SVG is recommended since it scales cleanly across screen sizes and resolutions without quality loss.

faq.question: How do I add spacing between the icon and text?
faq.answer: Set a margin value on the icon element itself, which pushes it away from the adjacent text block.

faq.question: Can I animate the button on hover?
faq.answer: Yes, the States dropdown lets you define a Hover state and tweak properties like background color for an interactive effect.
